% DESIGN PROCESS PHASES - AUTOMATICALLY GENERATED

% Phase: Define Problem
\definecolor{PhaseIDdefineproblem}{HTML}{3b82f6}
\csdef{phasecolor@1}{PhaseIDdefineproblem}
\csdef{phasename@1}{Define Problem}

% Phase: Generate Concepts
\definecolor{PhaseIDgenerateconcepts}{HTML}{a855f7}
\csdef{phasecolor@2}{PhaseIDgenerateconcepts}
\csdef{phasename@2}{Generate Concepts}

% Phase: Develop Solution
\definecolor{PhaseIDdevelopsolution}{HTML}{6366f1}
\csdef{phasecolor@3}{PhaseIDdevelopsolution}
\csdef{phasename@3}{Develop Solution}

% Phase: Construct and Test
\definecolor{PhaseIDconstructtest}{HTML}{f97316}
\csdef{phasecolor@4}{PhaseIDconstructtest}
\csdef{phasename@4}{Construct and Test}

% Phase: Evaluate Solution
\definecolor{PhaseIDevaluatesolution}{HTML}{10b981}
\csdef{phasecolor@5}{PhaseIDevaluatesolution}
\csdef{phasename@5}{Evaluate Solution}

\newcommand{\phaselist}{
    \notebookphase{PhaseIDdefineproblem}{Define Problem}{Identifying the core issue, setting SMART goals, outlining constraints and deliverables.}
    \notebookphase{PhaseIDgenerateconcepts}{Generate Concepts}{Brainstorming, research, prototyping, and decision matrices to evaluate potential solutions.}
    \notebookphase{PhaseIDdevelopsolution}{Develop Solution}{Creating CAD, detailed sketches, math calculations, graphical models, and pseudocode.}
    \notebookphase{PhaseIDconstructtest}{Construct and Test}{Building the robot, writing the code, executing test plans, and gathering qualitative/quantitative data.}
    \notebookphase{PhaseIDevaluatesolution}{Evaluate Solution}{Reflecting on constraints, event outcomes, and planning future improvements.}
}
